other_observers: Brandon Percival; Rich Miller in late afternoon of same day
duration: last photo taken was 7:50 and last seen at 8:00 P.M. with GBHEs
description: This was a wader by long legs, long neck and long bill. The bill was mostly dark but it did show pink at at the base and at the gape and was long and spear-like as in herons, egrets, and bitterns. The legs were dark gray. The irdes were yellowish, a good character for young Reddish Egrets (see photos). There was a thin whitish "V" that outlined the base of the bill. The crown, face, chin, throat, and hindneck was a reddish brown and it extended on both sides of the long neck most of the way to the body, but not all of the way. The lower neck, breast, and belly were creamy gray-white. The body was dull blue-gray with a few rufous feathers stuck in here and there. The legs were long and were blue gray in color.Behaviors: At times this bird was an active feeder and at times used the herky-jerky style of feeding, but it mostly employed a slow walking style of searching for prey and it also just stood and watched us for long periods.Call: none heardPlumage: immature probably juvenal
